title.zh: 卷三十二 張耳陳餘傳
title.en: Volume 32: Zhang Er and Chen Yu
title.raw: 卷三十二 張耳陳餘傳 Volume 32: Zhang Er and Chen Yu

sentences.zh: 張耳、陳餘

translations.literal: Zhang Er and Chen Yu.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Er and Chen Yu.

sentences.zh: 張耳，大梁人也，少時及魏公子毋忌為客。

translations.literal: Zhang Er was from Daliang. As a young man, he served among the retainers of the Wei prince Wuji.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Er was from Daliang. As a young man, he served among the retainers of the Wei prince Wuji.

sentences.zh: 嘗亡命遊外黃，外黃富人女甚美，庸奴其夫，亡邸父客。

translations.literal: While in exile in Waihuang, he became connected with a wealthy family whose daughter was famed for her beauty, though she had been badly matched in marriage.
translations.idiomatic: While in exile in Waihuang, he became connected with a wealthy family whose daughter was famed for her beauty, though she had been badly matched in marriage.

sentences.zh: 父客謂曰：「必欲求賢夫，從張耳。」

translations.literal: A retainer of her father told her, If you truly want a worthy husband, choose Zhang Er.
translations.idiomatic: A retainer of her father told her, If you truly want a worthy husband, choose Zhang Er.

sentences.zh: 女聽，為請決，嫁之。

translations.literal: She agreed, secured a separation, and married Zhang Er.
translations.idiomatic: She agreed, secured a separation, and married Zhang Er.

sentences.zh: 女家厚奉給耳，耳以故致千里客，宦為外黃令。

translations.literal: Her family funded him lavishly. With those resources he gathered talented followers from far and wide and eventually became magistrate of Waihuang.
translations.idiomatic: Her family funded him lavishly. With those resources he gathered talented followers from far and wide and eventually became magistrate of Waihuang.

sentences.zh: 陳餘，亦大梁人，好儒術。

translations.literal: Chen Yu, also from Daliang, was devoted to classical learning.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Yu, also from Daliang, was devoted to classical learning.

sentences.zh: 遊趙苦陘，富人公乘氏以其女妻之。

translations.literal: While in Zhao at Kujing, he married the daughter of the wealthy Gongcheng family.
translations.idiomatic: While in Zhao at Kujing, he married the daughter of the wealthy Gongcheng family.

sentences.zh: 餘年少，父事耳，相與為刎頸交。

translations.literal: Though younger, Chen Yu treated Zhang Er like a father, and the two became sworn friends willing to die for each other.
translations.idiomatic: Though younger, Chen Yu treated Zhang Er like a father, and the two became sworn friends willing to die for each other.

sentences.zh: 高祖為布衣時，嘗從耳遊。

translations.literal: Before he rose to power, Gaozu had once traveled with Zhang Er.
translations.idiomatic: Before he rose to power, Gaozu had once traveled with Zhang Er.

sentences.zh: 秦滅魏，購求耳千金，餘五百金。

translations.literal: After Qin conquered Wei, bounties were posted: one thousand jin for Zhang Er and five hundred for Chen Yu.
translations.idiomatic: After Qin conquered Wei, bounties were posted: one thousand jin for Zhang Er and five hundred for Chen Yu.

sentences.zh: 兩人變名姓，俱之陳，為里監門。

translations.literal: They changed their identities, fled to Chen, and worked as local gatekeepers.
translations.idiomatic: They changed their identities, fled to Chen, and worked as local gatekeepers.

sentences.zh: 吏嘗以過笞餘，餘欲起，耳攝使受笞。

translations.literal: Once, when an official beat Chen Yu for a minor fault, Chen Yu tried to resist, but Zhang Er restrained him and made him endure it.
translations.idiomatic: Once, when an official beat Chen Yu for a minor fault, Chen Yu tried to resist, but Zhang Er restrained him and made him endure it.

sentences.zh: 吏去，耳數之曰：「始吾與公言何如？

translations.literal: After the official departed, Zhang Er scolded him and asked what he had told him from the beginning.
translations.idiomatic: After the official departed, Zhang Er scolded him and asked what he had told him from the beginning.

sentences.zh: 今見小辱而欲死一吏乎？」

translations.literal: He asked whether Chen Yu would throw his life away over a petty humiliation.
translations.idiomatic: He asked whether Chen Yu would throw his life away over a petty humiliation.

sentences.zh: 餘謝罪。

translations.literal: Chen Yu apologized.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Yu apologized.

sentences.zh: 陳涉起蘄至陳，耳、餘上謁涉。

translations.literal: When Chen She's uprising reached Chen, Zhang Er and Chen Yu came to pay their respects.
translations.idiomatic: When Chen She's uprising reached Chen, Zhang Er and Chen Yu came to pay their respects.

sentences.zh: 涉及左右生平數聞耳、餘賢，見，大喜。

translations.literal: Chen She and his aides had long heard of their reputation; when he met them, he was delighted.
translations.idiomatic: Chen She and his aides had long heard of their reputation; when he met them, he was delighted.

sentences.zh: 陳豪桀說涉曰：「將軍被堅執銳，帥士卒以誅暴秦，復立楚社稷，功德宜為王。」

translations.literal: A prominent man of Chen urged Chen She to claim kingship, saying he had armed himself, led troops against brutal Qin, and revived Chu.
translations.idiomatic: A prominent man of Chen urged Chen She to claim kingship, saying he had armed himself, led troops against brutal Qin, and revived Chu.

sentences.zh: 陳涉問兩人，兩人對曰：「將軍瞋目張膽，出萬死不顧之計，為天下除殘。

translations.literal: Chen She asked Zhang Er and Chen Yu. They said he had taken the boldest risks to remove tyranny from the realm.
translations.idiomatic: Chen She asked Zhang Er and Chen Yu. They said he had taken the boldest risks to remove tyranny from the realm.

sentences.zh: 今始至陳而王之，視天下私。

translations.literal: But they warned that if he proclaimed himself king immediately, all under Heaven would see it as private ambition.
translations.idiomatic: But they warned that if he proclaimed himself king immediately, all under Heaven would see it as private ambition.

sentences.zh: 願將軍毋王，急引兵而西，遣人立六國後，自為樹黨。

translations.literal: They advised him not to take kingship yet, but to march west, restore heirs of the six states, and build alliances.
translations.idiomatic: They advised him not to take kingship yet, but to march west, restore heirs of the six states, and build alliances.

sentences.zh: 如此，野無交兵，誅暴秦，據咸陽以令諸侯，則帝業成矣。

translations.literal: Then he could defeat Qin, seize Xianyang, command the feudal lords, and complete an imperial enterprise.
translations.idiomatic: Then he could defeat Qin, seize Xianyang, command the feudal lords, and complete an imperial enterprise.

sentences.zh: 今獨王陳，恐天下解矣。」

translations.literal: If he made only Chen his kingdom at once, they feared the movement would splinter.
translations.idiomatic: If he made only Chen his kingdom at once, they feared the movement would splinter.

sentences.zh: 涉不聽，遂立為王。

translations.literal: Chen She did not accept their advice and declared himself king.
translations.idiomatic: Chen She did not accept their advice and declared himself king.

sentences.zh: 耳、餘復說陳王曰：「大王興梁、楚，務在入關，未及收河北也。

translations.literal: Zhang Er and Chen Yu again advised him that Hebei was still unsecured and his priority remained entering the passes.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Er and Chen Yu again advised him that Hebei was still unsecured and his priority remained entering the passes.

sentences.zh: 臣嘗遊趙，知其豪桀，願請奇兵略趙地。」

translations.literal: They said they knew Zhao's local strongmen and asked to take a special force to seize Zhao territory.
translations.idiomatic: They said they knew Zhao's local strongmen and asked to take a special force to seize Zhao territory.

sentences.zh: 於是陳王許之，以所善陳人武臣為將軍，耳、餘為左右校尉，與卒三千人，從白馬渡河。

translations.literal: He agreed, appointing his trusted countryman Wu Chen as commander, with Zhang Er and Chen Yu as deputy commandants. They crossed at Baima with three thousand troops.
translations.idiomatic: He agreed, appointing his trusted countryman Wu Chen as commander, with Zhang Er and Chen Yu as deputy commandants. They crossed at Baima with three thousand troops.

sentences.zh: 至諸縣，說其豪桀曰：「秦為亂政虐刑，殘滅天下，北為長城之役，南有五領之戍，外內騷動，百姓罷敝，頭會箕斂以供軍費，財匱力盡，重以苛法，使天下父子不相聊。

translations.literal: As they advanced, they told local elites that Qin's violent rule had ruined the realm with forced labor, harsh levies, exhausted households, and brutal law.
translations.idiomatic: As they advanced, they told local elites that Qin's violent rule had ruined the realm with forced labor, harsh levies, exhausted households, and brutal law.

sentences.zh: 今陳王奮臂為天下倡始，莫不嚮應，家自為怒，各報其怨，縣殺其令丞，郡殺其守尉。

translations.literal: They said Chen She had raised the banner for all under Heaven and everywhere people were responding by killing Qin officials.
translations.idiomatic: They said Chen She had raised the banner for all under Heaven and everywhere people were responding by killing Qin officials.

sentences.zh: 今以張大楚，王陳，使吳廣、周文將卒百萬西擊秦。

translations.literal: They added that Chu had been restored and huge armies were already striking west at Qin.
translations.idiomatic: They added that Chu had been restored and huge armies were already striking west at Qin.

sentences.zh: 於此時而不成封侯之業者，非人豪也。

translations.literal: At such a moment, they said, anyone who failed to win a marquisate was no true hero.
translations.idiomatic: At such a moment, they said, anyone who failed to win a marquisate was no true hero.

sentences.zh: 夫因天下之力而攻無道之君，報父兄之怨而成割地之業，此一時也。」

translations.literal: To use the realm's strength against a lawless ruler, avenge one's kin, and carve out a domain was a once-in-an-age chance.
translations.idiomatic: To use the realm's strength against a lawless ruler, avenge one's kin, and carve out a domain was a once-in-an-age chance.

sentences.zh: 豪桀皆然其言。

translations.literal: The local strongmen accepted their argument.
translations.idiomatic: The local strongmen accepted their argument.

sentences.zh: 乃行收兵，得數萬人，號武信君。

translations.literal: They raised forces as they went, gathering tens of thousands, and Wu Chen took the title Lord Wuxin.
translations.idiomatic: They raised forces as they went, gathering tens of thousands, and Wu Chen took the title Lord Wuxin.

sentences.zh: 下趙十餘城，餘皆城守莫肯下。

translations.literal: They captured over ten Zhao cities, while the rest held behind their walls.
translations.idiomatic: They captured over ten Zhao cities, while the rest held behind their walls.

sentences.zh: 乃引兵東北擊范陽。

translations.literal: Then they marched northeast on Fanyang.
translations.idiomatic: Then they marched northeast on Fanyang.

sentences.zh: 范陽人蒯通說其令徐公降武信君，又說武信君以侯印封范陽令。

translations.literal: A Fanyang native, Kuai Tong, persuaded Magistrate Xu to surrender and convinced Lord Wuxin to reward him with a marquis seal.
translations.idiomatic: A Fanyang native, Kuai Tong, persuaded Magistrate Xu to surrender and convinced Lord Wuxin to reward him with a marquis seal.

sentences.zh: 語在通傳。

translations.literal: The full exchange is recorded in Kuai Tong's biography.
translations.idiomatic: The full exchange is recorded in Kuai Tong's biography.

sentences.zh: 趙地聞之，不戰下者三十餘城。

translations.literal: After news spread, more than thirty Zhao cities surrendered without fighting.
translations.idiomatic: After news spread, more than thirty Zhao cities surrendered without fighting.

sentences.zh: 至邯鄲，耳、餘聞周章軍入關，至戲卻；

translations.literal: At Handan, Zhang Er and Chen Yu heard that Zhou Zhang had entered the passes but been repulsed at Xi.
translations.idiomatic: At Handan, Zhang Er and Chen Yu heard that Zhou Zhang had entered the passes but been repulsed at Xi.

sentences.zh: 又聞諸將為陳王徇地，多以讒毀得罪誅。

translations.literal: They also heard that many commanders serving Chen She were ruined by slander and executed.
translations.idiomatic: They also heard that many commanders serving Chen She were ruined by slander and executed.

sentences.zh: 怨陳王不以為將軍而以為校尉，乃說武臣曰：「陳王非必立六國後。

translations.literal: Resentful that they had only deputy rank, they told Wu Chen that Chen She could not be trusted to restore the six states.
translations.idiomatic: Resentful that they had only deputy rank, they told Wu Chen that Chen She could not be trusted to restore the six states.

sentences.zh: 今將軍下趙數十城，獨介居河北，不王無以填之。

translations.literal: They said Wu Chen now held dozens of Zhao cities in Hebei and could not stabilize them without kingship.
translations.idiomatic: They said Wu Chen now held dozens of Zhao cities in Hebei and could not stabilize them without kingship.

sentences.zh: 且陳王聽讒，還報，恐不得脫於禍。

translations.literal: They warned that Chen She listened to slander and that reporting back might be fatal.
translations.idiomatic: They warned that Chen She listened to slander and that reporting back might be fatal.

sentences.zh: 願將軍毋失時。」

translations.literal: They urged him not to miss the moment.
translations.idiomatic: They urged him not to miss the moment.

sentences.zh: 武臣乃聽，遂立為趙王。

translations.literal: Wu Chen accepted and proclaimed himself King of Zhao.
translations.idiomatic: Wu Chen accepted and proclaimed himself King of Zhao.

sentences.zh: 以餘為大將軍，耳為丞相。

translations.literal: He appointed Chen Yu grand general and Zhang Er chancellor.
translations.idiomatic: He appointed Chen Yu grand general and Zhang Er chancellor.

sentences.zh: 使人報陳王，陳王大怒，欲盡族武臣等家，而發兵擊趙。

translations.literal: When news reached Chen, Chen She was enraged and wanted to annihilate Wu Chen's families and attack Zhao.
translations.idiomatic: When news reached Chen, Chen She was enraged and wanted to annihilate Wu Chen's families and attack Zhao.

sentences.zh: 相國房君諫曰：「秦未亡，今又誅武臣等家，此生一秦也。

translations.literal: Chancellor Fang remonstrated that Qin still stood and such punishment would create a second Qin.
translations.idiomatic: Chancellor Fang remonstrated that Qin still stood and such punishment would create a second Qin.

sentences.zh: 不如因而賀之，使急引兵西擊秦。」

translations.literal: He said it was better to accept the situation, congratulate Zhao, and press them to attack Qin in the west.
translations.idiomatic: He said it was better to accept the situation, congratulate Zhao, and press them to attack Qin in the west.

sentences.zh: 陳王從其計，徙繫武臣等家宮中，封耳子敖為成都君。

translations.literal: Chen She accepted: he kept their families under palace custody and enfeoffed Zhang Er's son Ao as Lord of Chengdu.
translations.idiomatic: Chen She accepted: he kept their families under palace custody and enfeoffed Zhang Er's son Ao as Lord of Chengdu.

sentences.zh: 使使者賀趙，趣兵西入關。

translations.literal: He sent an envoy to congratulate Zhao and urge immediate westward operations into the passes.
translations.idiomatic: He sent an envoy to congratulate Zhao and urge immediate westward operations into the passes.

sentences.zh: 耳、餘說武臣曰：「王王趙，非楚意，特以計賀王。

translations.literal: Zhang Er and Chen Yu warned Wu Chen that Chu's congratulations were tactical and not sincere.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Er and Chen Yu warned Wu Chen that Chu's congratulations were tactical and not sincere.

sentences.zh: 楚已滅秦，必加兵於趙。

translations.literal: They said Chu would certainly turn on Zhao once Qin was defeated.
translations.idiomatic: They said Chu would certainly turn on Zhao once Qin was defeated.

sentences.zh: 願王毋西兵，北徇燕、代，南收河內，以自廣。

translations.literal: They advised him not to go west but to expand north into Yan and Dai and secure land south of the river.
translations.idiomatic: They advised him not to go west but to expand north into Yan and Dai and secure land south of the river.

sentences.zh: 趙南據大河，北有燕、代，楚雖勝秦，必不敢制趙。」

translations.literal: With the Great River in front and Yan and Dai behind, they argued, Zhao would be hard to subdue even after Chu's victory.
translations.idiomatic: With the Great River in front and Yan and Dai behind, they argued, Zhao would be hard to subdue even after Chu's victory.

sentences.zh: 趙王以為然，因不西兵，而使韓廣略燕，李良略常山，張黶略上黨。

translations.literal: The King of Zhao agreed, kept troops from the western front, and sent commanders into Yan, Changshan, and Shangdang.
translations.idiomatic: The King of Zhao agreed, kept troops from the western front, and sent commanders into Yan, Changshan, and Shangdang.

sentences.zh: 韓廣至燕，燕人因立廣為燕王。

translations.literal: When Han Guang arrived, the Yan people proclaimed him King of Yan.
translations.idiomatic: When Han Guang arrived, the Yan people proclaimed him King of Yan.

sentences.zh: 趙王乃與陳、餘北略地燕界。

translations.literal: The Zhao king then moved north with Zhang Er and Chen Yu to secure territory on Yan's frontier.
translations.idiomatic: The Zhao king then moved north with Zhang Er and Chen Yu to secure territory on Yan's frontier.

sentences.zh: 趙王間出，為燕軍所得。

translations.literal: During one excursion, the Zhao king was captured by Yan forces.
translations.idiomatic: During one excursion, the Zhao king was captured by Yan forces.

sentences.zh: 燕囚之，欲與分地。

translations.literal: Yan imprisoned him and demanded a partition of land.
translations.idiomatic: Yan imprisoned him and demanded a partition of land.

sentences.zh: 使者往，燕輒殺之，以固求地。

translations.literal: Each envoy sent to negotiate was killed by Yan to harden its demands.
translations.idiomatic: Each envoy sent to negotiate was killed by Yan to harden its demands.

sentences.zh: 耳、餘患之。

translations.literal: Zhang Er and Chen Yu were deeply troubled.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Er and Chen Yu were deeply troubled.

sentences.zh: 有廝養卒謝其舍曰：「吾為二公說燕，與趙王載歸。」

translations.literal: A low-ranking stable-hand volunteered to negotiate and promised to bring the king back.
translations.idiomatic: A low-ranking stable-hand volunteered to negotiate and promised to bring the king back.

sentences.zh: 舍中人皆笑曰：「使者往十輩皆死，若何以能得王？」

translations.literal: Everyone laughed and said many envoys had already died, so how could he succeed.
translations.idiomatic: Everyone laughed and said many envoys had already died, so how could he succeed.

sentences.zh: 乃走燕壁。

translations.literal: He went straight to the Yan camp.
translations.idiomatic: He went straight to the Yan camp.

sentences.zh: 燕將見之，問曰：「知臣何欲？」

translations.literal: A Yan general summoned him and asked whether he knew what Yan wanted.
translations.idiomatic: A Yan general summoned him and asked whether he knew what Yan wanted.

sentences.zh: 燕將曰：「若欲得王耳。」

translations.literal: The general said he wanted only the Zhao king.
translations.idiomatic: The general said he wanted only the Zhao king.

sentences.zh: 曰：「君知張耳、陳餘何如人也？」

translations.literal: The soldier asked whether he truly understood Zhang Er and Chen Yu.
translations.idiomatic: The soldier asked whether he truly understood Zhang Er and Chen Yu.

sentences.zh: 燕將曰：「賢人也。」

translations.literal: The general replied that they were worthy men.
translations.idiomatic: The general replied that they were worthy men.

sentences.zh: 曰：「其志何欲？」

translations.literal: The soldier asked what he thought they wanted.
translations.idiomatic: The soldier asked what he thought they wanted.

sentences.zh: 燕將曰：「欲得其王耳。」

translations.literal: The general said they wanted their king back.
translations.idiomatic: The general said they wanted their king back.

sentences.zh: 趙卒笑曰；

translations.literal: The Zhao soldier laughed.
translations.idiomatic: The Zhao soldier laughed.

sentences.zh: 「君未知兩人所欲也。

translations.literal: He said the general did not understand what the two men really wanted.
translations.idiomatic: He said the general did not understand what the two men really wanted.

sentences.zh: 夫武臣、張耳、陳餘，杖馬箠下趙數十城，亦各欲南面而王。

translations.literal: Wu Chen, Zhang Er, and Chen Yu had taken dozens of cities by force, and each had ambitions to rule.
translations.idiomatic: Wu Chen, Zhang Er, and Chen Yu had taken dozens of cities by force, and each had ambitions to rule.

sentences.zh: 夫臣之與主，豈可同日道哉！

translations.literal: Ruler and minister, he said, do not stay on the same path forever.
translations.idiomatic: Ruler and minister, he said, do not stay on the same path forever.

sentences.zh: 顧其勢初定，且以長少先立武臣，以持趙心。

translations.literal: They had only backed Wu Chen first to stabilize Zhao while things were unsettled.
translations.idiomatic: They had only backed Wu Chen first to stabilize Zhao while things were unsettled.

sentences.zh: 今趙地已服，兩人亦欲分趙而王，時未可耳。

translations.literal: Now that Zhao was largely secure, they too wanted to divide and rule it, but were waiting for the right time.
translations.idiomatic: Now that Zhao was largely secure, they too wanted to divide and rule it, but were waiting for the right time.

sentences.zh: 今君囚趙王，念此兩人名為求王，實欲燕殺之，此兩人分趙而王。

translations.literal: If Yan kept the Zhao king captive, they might publicly demand him yet privately hope Yan killed him so they could split Zhao.
translations.idiomatic: If Yan kept the Zhao king captive, they might publicly demand him yet privately hope Yan killed him so they could split Zhao.

sentences.zh: 夫以一趙尚易燕，況以兩賢王左提右挈，而責殺王，滅燕易矣。」

translations.literal: One Zhao was already dangerous to Yan; with two capable rulers blaming Yan for regicide, Yan would be easy to destroy.
translations.idiomatic: One Zhao was already dangerous to Yan; with two capable rulers blaming Yan for regicide, Yan would be easy to destroy.

sentences.zh: 燕以為然，乃歸趙王。

translations.literal: Yan accepted this reasoning and released the Zhao king.
translations.idiomatic: Yan accepted this reasoning and released the Zhao king.

sentences.zh: 養卒為御而歸。

translations.literal: The stable-hand himself drove the king back.
translations.idiomatic: The stable-hand himself drove the king back.

sentences.zh: 李良已定常山，還報趙王，趙王復使良略太原。

translations.literal: After pacifying Changshan, Li Liang reported back, and the Zhao king sent him on to take Taiyuan.
translations.idiomatic: After pacifying Changshan, Li Liang reported back, and the Zhao king sent him on to take Taiyuan.

sentences.zh: 至石邑，秦兵塞井陘，未能前。

translations.literal: At Shiyi, Qin forces blocked Jingxing Pass and stopped his advance.
translations.idiomatic: At Shiyi, Qin forces blocked Jingxing Pass and stopped his advance.

sentences.zh: 秦將詐稱二世使使遺良書，不封，曰：「良嘗事我，得顯幸，誠能反趙為秦，赦良罪，貴良。」

translations.literal: A Qin commander sent an unsealed forged letter in the Second Emperor's name, promising pardon and reward if Li Liang turned Zhao back to Qin.
translations.idiomatic: A Qin commander sent an unsealed forged letter in the Second Emperor's name, promising pardon and reward if Li Liang turned Zhao back to Qin.

sentences.zh: 良得書，疑不信，之邯鄲益請兵。

translations.literal: Li Liang read it with suspicion and headed to Handan to request reinforcements.
translations.idiomatic: Li Liang read it with suspicion and headed to Handan to request reinforcements.

sentences.zh: 未至，道逢趙王姊，從百餘騎。

translations.literal: Before reaching Handan, he met the Zhao king's elder sister traveling with over a hundred horsemen.
translations.idiomatic: Before reaching Handan, he met the Zhao king's elder sister traveling with over a hundred horsemen.

sentences.zh: 良望見，以為王，伏謁道旁。

translations.literal: Seeing the procession from a distance, he thought the king was present and knelt in formal greeting.
translations.idiomatic: Seeing the procession from a distance, he thought the king was present and knelt in formal greeting.

sentences.zh: 王姊醉，不知其將，使騎謝良。

translations.literal: The princess, drunk, did not recognize him and had her riders dismiss him.
translations.idiomatic: The princess, drunk, did not recognize him and had her riders dismiss him.

sentences.zh: 良素貴，起，慚其從官。

translations.literal: Li Liang, normally treated with deference, stood up humiliated before his subordinates.
translations.idiomatic: Li Liang, normally treated with deference, stood up humiliated before his subordinates.

sentences.zh: 從官有一人曰：「天下叛秦，能者先立。

translations.literal: One officer said the whole realm was in revolt and the able should establish themselves first.
translations.idiomatic: One officer said the whole realm was in revolt and the able should establish themselves first.

sentences.zh: 且趙王素出將軍下，今女兒乃不為將軍下車，請追殺之。」

translations.literal: He added that even a woman now refused proper respect to Li Liang and urged him to pursue and kill her.
translations.idiomatic: He added that even a woman now refused proper respect to Li Liang and urged him to pursue and kill her.

sentences.zh: 良以得秦書，欲反趙，未決，因此怒，遣人追殺王姊，遂襲邯鄲。

translations.literal: Already wavering because of Qin's letter, Li Liang acted in anger, had the princess killed, and then attacked Handan.
translations.idiomatic: Already wavering because of Qin's letter, Li Liang acted in anger, had the princess killed, and then attacked Handan.

sentences.zh: 邯鄲不知，竟殺武臣。

translations.literal: Handan was caught off guard, and Wu Chen was ultimately killed.
translations.idiomatic: Handan was caught off guard, and Wu Chen was ultimately killed.

sentences.zh: 趙人多為耳、餘耳目者，故得脫出。

translations.literal: Because many locals informed for Zhang Er and Chen Yu, they managed to escape.
translations.idiomatic: Because many locals informed for Zhang Er and Chen Yu, they managed to escape.

sentences.zh: 收兵得數萬人。

translations.literal: They regrouped and raised tens of thousands of troops.
translations.idiomatic: They regrouped and raised tens of thousands of troops.

sentences.zh: 客有說耳、餘曰：「兩君羈旅，而欲附趙，難可獨立；

translations.literal: A retainer told them they were still outsiders in Zhao and could not easily stand on their own.
translations.idiomatic: A retainer told them they were still outsiders in Zhao and could not easily stand on their own.

sentences.zh: 趙後，輔以誼，可就功。」

translations.literal: He advised them to find a surviving Zhao heir and support him in the name of legitimacy.
translations.idiomatic: He advised them to find a surviving Zhao heir and support him in the name of legitimacy.

sentences.zh: 及求得趙歇，立為趙王，居信都。

translations.literal: They located Zhao Xie, enthroned him as King of Zhao, and set his court at Xindu.
translations.idiomatic: They located Zhao Xie, enthroned him as King of Zhao, and set his court at Xindu.

sentences.zh: 李良進兵擊餘，餘敗良。

translations.literal: Li Liang advanced and attacked Chen Yu, but Chen Yu defeated him.
translations.idiomatic: Li Liang advanced and attacked Chen Yu, but Chen Yu defeated him.

sentences.zh: 良走歸章邯。

translations.literal: Li Liang fled and went over to Zhang Han.
translations.idiomatic: Li Liang fled and went over to Zhang Han.

sentences.zh: 章邯引兵至邯鄲，皆徙其民河內，夷其城郭。

translations.literal: Zhang Han brought his army to Handan, moved its people to Henei, and leveled the city walls.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Han brought his army to Handan, moved its people to Henei, and leveled the city walls.

sentences.zh: 耳與趙王歇走入鉅鹿城，王離圍之。

translations.literal: Zhang Er and King Xie of Zhao fled into Julu, where Wang Li besieged them.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Er and King Xie of Zhao fled into Julu, where Wang Li besieged them.

sentences.zh: 餘北收常山兵，得數萬人，軍鉅鹿北。

translations.literal: Chen Yu gathered Changshan troops in the north, gained tens of thousands, and camped north of Julu.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Yu gathered Changshan troops in the north, gained tens of thousands, and camped north of Julu.

sentences.zh: 章邯軍鉅鹿南棘原，築甬道屬河，饟王離。

translations.literal: Zhang Han camped south of Julu at Jiyuan, built a supply corridor to the river, and provisioned Wang Li.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Han camped south of Julu at Jiyuan, built a supply corridor to the river, and provisioned Wang Li.

sentences.zh: 王離兵食多，急攻鉅鹿。

translations.literal: Wang Li had ample supplies and pressed the assault on Julu.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Li had ample supplies and pressed the assault on Julu.

sentences.zh: 鉅鹿城中食盡，耳數使人召餘，餘自度兵少，不能敵秦，不敢前。

translations.literal: Food inside Julu ran out. Zhang Er repeatedly summoned Chen Yu, but Chen Yu judged his forces too small to face Qin and would not advance.
translations.idiomatic: Food inside Julu ran out. Zhang Er repeatedly summoned Chen Yu, but Chen Yu judged his forces too small to face Qin and would not advance.

sentences.zh: 數月，耳大怒，怨餘，使張黶、陳釋往讓餘曰：「始吾與公為刎頸交，今王與耳旦暮死，而公擁兵數萬，不肯相救，相不赴奏俱死？

translations.literal: After months, Zhang Er grew furious and sent Zhang Yan and Chen Shi to rebuke Chen Yu, saying that king and Zhang Er were near death while Chen Yu held tens of thousands of troops and would not rescue them.
translations.idiomatic: After months, Zhang Er grew furious and sent Zhang Yan and Chen Shi to rebuke Chen Yu, saying that king and Zhang Er were near death while Chen Yu held tens of thousands of troops and would not rescue them.

sentences.zh: 且什一二相全。」

translations.literal: They said that even saving one or two in ten would still be something.
translations.idiomatic: They said that even saving one or two in ten would still be something.

sentences.zh: 餘曰：「所以不俱死，欲為趙王、張君報秦。

translations.literal: Chen Yu replied that he was not dying with them because he still wished to avenge Zhao and Zhang against Qin.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Yu replied that he was not dying with them because he still wished to avenge Zhao and Zhang against Qin.

sentences.zh: 今俱死，如以肉餧虎，何益？」

translations.literal: If all died together now, he said, it would be like throwing meat to a tiger, with no gain.
translations.idiomatic: If all died together now, he said, it would be like throwing meat to a tiger, with no gain.

sentences.zh: 張黶、陳釋曰：「事已急，要以俱死立信，安知後慮！」

translations.literal: Zhang Yan and Chen Shi said the crisis required dying together to keep faith, not planning for a distant future.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Yan and Chen Shi said the crisis required dying together to keep faith, not planning for a distant future.

sentences.zh: 餘曰：「吾顧以無益。」

translations.literal: Chen Yu answered that he saw no benefit in that.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Yu answered that he saw no benefit in that.

sentences.zh: 乃使五千人令張黶、陳釋先嘗秦軍，至皆沒。

translations.literal: He then sent five thousand men under Zhang Yan and Chen Shi to test Qin first; all were lost.
translations.idiomatic: He then sent five thousand men under Zhang Yan and Chen Shi to test Qin first; all were lost.

sentences.zh: 當是時，燕、齊、楚聞趙急，皆來救。

translations.literal: At that time, Yan, Qi, and Chu all heard Zhao was in danger and came to aid it.
translations.idiomatic: At that time, Yan, Qi, and Chu all heard Zhao was in danger and came to aid it.

sentences.zh: 張敖亦北收代，得萬餘人來，皆壁餘旁。

translations.literal: Zhang Ao also gathered over ten thousand in Dai and arrived, all camping beside Chen Yu.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Ao also gathered over ten thousand in Dai and arrived, all camping beside Chen Yu.

sentences.zh: 項羽兵數絕章邯甬道，王離軍乏食。

translations.literal: Xiang Yu repeatedly cut Zhang Han's supply corridor, and Wang Li's forces ran short of food.
translations.idiomatic: Xiang Yu repeatedly cut Zhang Han's supply corridor, and Wang Li's forces ran short of food.

sentences.zh: 項羽悉引兵渡河，破章邯軍。

translations.literal: Xiang Yu then led all forces across the river and broke Zhang Han's army.
translations.idiomatic: Xiang Yu then led all forces across the river and broke Zhang Han's army.

sentences.zh: 諸侯軍及敢擊秦軍，遂虜王離。

translations.literal: The feudal armies then dared attack Qin and captured Wang Li.
translations.idiomatic: The feudal armies then dared attack Qin and captured Wang Li.

sentences.zh: 於是趙王歇、張耳得出鉅鹿。

translations.literal: King Xie of Zhao and Zhang Er were thus able to emerge from Julu.
translations.idiomatic: King Xie of Zhao and Zhang Er were thus able to emerge from Julu.

sentences.zh: 與餘相見，責讓餘，問張黶、陳釋所在。

translations.literal: When they met Chen Yu, they reproached him and asked where Zhang Yan and Chen Shi were.
translations.idiomatic: When they met Chen Yu, they reproached him and asked where Zhang Yan and Chen Shi were.

sentences.zh: 餘曰：「黶、釋以必死責臣，臣使將五千人先嘗秦軍，皆沒。」

translations.literal: Chen Yu said they had demanded certain death, so he sent them with five thousand men to test Qin first, and all perished.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Yu said they had demanded certain death, so he sent them with five thousand men to test Qin first, and all perished.

sentences.zh: 耳不信，以為殺之，數問餘。

translations.literal: Zhang Er did not believe him and thought Chen Yu had killed them, questioning him repeatedly.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Er did not believe him and thought Chen Yu had killed them, questioning him repeatedly.

sentences.zh: 餘怒曰：「不意君之望臣深也！

translations.literal: Chen Yu angrily replied that he had not expected Zhang Er to distrust him so deeply.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Yu angrily replied that he had not expected Zhang Er to distrust him so deeply.

sentences.zh: 豈以臣重去將哉？」

translations.literal: He asked whether he would really have cast away his own commanders for no reason.
translations.idiomatic: He asked whether he would really have cast away his own commanders for no reason.

sentences.zh: 乃脫解印綬與耳，耳不敢受。

translations.literal: He then removed his seal and sash and handed them to Zhang Er.
translations.idiomatic: He then removed his seal and sash and handed them to Zhang Er.

sentences.zh: 餘起如廁，客有說耳曰：「天予不取，反受其咎。

translations.literal: Zhang Er did not dare accept. While Chen Yu stepped out, a retainer advised Zhang Er that refusing Heaven's gift would invite disaster.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Er did not dare accept. While Chen Yu stepped out, a retainer advised Zhang Er that refusing Heaven's gift would invite disaster.

sentences.zh: 今陳將軍與君印綬，不受，反天不祥。

translations.literal: The retainer said Chen Yu was offering the seal now, and refusing it would be an ill omen.
translations.idiomatic: The retainer said Chen Yu was offering the seal now, and refusing it would be an ill omen.

sentences.zh: 急取之。」

translations.literal: He urged Zhang Er to take it at once.
translations.idiomatic: He urged Zhang Er to take it at once.

sentences.zh: 耳乃佩其印，收其麾下。

translations.literal: Zhang Er then wore the seal and took over Chen Yu's troops.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Er then wore the seal and took over Chen Yu's troops.

sentences.zh: 餘還，亦望耳不讓，趨出。

translations.literal: When Chen Yu returned and saw Zhang Er had not yielded, he withdrew in anger.
translations.idiomatic: When Chen Yu returned and saw Zhang Er had not yielded, he withdrew in anger.

sentences.zh: 耳遂收其兵。

translations.literal: Zhang Er then fully absorbed his forces.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Er then fully absorbed his forces.

sentences.zh: 餘獨與麾下數百人之河上澤中漁獵。

translations.literal: Chen Yu remained only with a few hundred followers, fishing and hunting in marshes by the river.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Yu remained only with a few hundred followers, fishing and hunting in marshes by the river.

sentences.zh: 由此有隙。

translations.literal: From this point, a deep rift formed between them.
translations.idiomatic: From this point, a deep rift formed between them.

sentences.zh: 趙王歇復居信都。

translations.literal: King Xie of Zhao returned to reside at Xindu.
translations.idiomatic: King Xie of Zhao returned to reside at Xindu.

sentences.zh: 耳從項羽入關。

translations.literal: Zhang Er entered the passes with Xiang Yu.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Er entered the passes with Xiang Yu.

sentences.zh: 項羽立諸侯，耳雅遊，多為人所稱。

translations.literal: When Xiang Yu enfeoffed the lords, Zhang Er was widely praised because he had long-standing connections.
translations.idiomatic: When Xiang Yu enfeoffed the lords, Zhang Er was widely praised because he had long-standing connections.

sentences.zh: 項羽素亦聞耳賢，乃分趙立耳為常山王，治信都。

translations.literal: Xiang Yu had also heard of Zhang Er's worth and therefore made him King of Changshan, ruling at Xindu.
translations.idiomatic: Xiang Yu had also heard of Zhang Er's worth and therefore made him King of Changshan, ruling at Xindu.

sentences.zh: 信都更名襄國。

translations.literal: Xindu was renamed Xiangguo.
translations.idiomatic: Xindu was renamed Xiangguo.

sentences.zh: 餘客多說項羽：「陳餘、張耳一體有功於趙。」

translations.literal: Many of Chen Yu's followers told Xiang Yu that Chen Yu and Zhang Er had jointly earned merit for Zhao.
translations.idiomatic: Many of Chen Yu's followers told Xiang Yu that Chen Yu and Zhang Er had jointly earned merit for Zhao.

sentences.zh: 羽以餘不從入關，聞其在南皮，即以南皮旁三縣封之。

translations.literal: Because Chen Yu had not entered the passes, Xiang Yu only granted him three counties near Nanpi when he heard he was there.
translations.idiomatic: Because Chen Yu had not entered the passes, Xiang Yu only granted him three counties near Nanpi when he heard he was there.

sentences.zh: 而徙趙王歇王代。

translations.literal: He moved King Xie of Zhao to rule Dai instead.
translations.idiomatic: He moved King Xie of Zhao to rule Dai instead.

sentences.zh: 耳之國，餘愈怒曰：「耳與餘功等也，今耳王，餘獨侯。」

translations.literal: When Zhang Er took his state, Chen Yu grew even angrier, saying their merits were equal yet Zhang Er was king while he was only a marquis.
translations.idiomatic: When Zhang Er took his state, Chen Yu grew even angrier, saying their merits were equal yet Zhang Er was king while he was only a marquis.

sentences.zh: 及齊王田榮叛楚，餘乃使夏說說田榮曰：「項羽為天下宰不平，盡王諸將善地，徙故王王惡地，今趙王乃居代！

translations.literal: When Qi's king Tian Rong rebelled against Chu, Chen Yu sent Xia Yue to persuade Tian Rong that Xiang Yu ruled the realm unjustly, giving good lands to his own generals and moving former kings to bad lands.
translations.idiomatic: When Qi's king Tian Rong rebelled against Chu, Chen Yu sent Xia Yue to persuade Tian Rong that Xiang Yu ruled the realm unjustly, giving good lands to his own generals and moving former kings to bad lands.

sentences.zh: 願王假臣兵，請以南皮為扞蔽。」

translations.literal: Chen Yu asked to borrow troops, offering Nanpi as a defensive screen.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Yu asked to borrow troops, offering Nanpi as a defensive screen.

sentences.zh: 田榮欲樹黨，乃遣兵從餘。

translations.literal: Tian Rong wanted to build his own faction and sent troops to aid Chen Yu.
translations.idiomatic: Tian Rong wanted to build his own faction and sent troops to aid Chen Yu.

sentences.zh: 餘悉三縣兵，襲常山王耳。

translations.literal: Chen Yu gathered all forces from his three counties and struck Zhang Er, King of Changshan.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Yu gathered all forces from his three counties and struck Zhang Er, King of Changshan.

sentences.zh: 耳敗走，曰：「漢王與我有故，而項王彊，立我，我欲之楚。」

translations.literal: Defeated, Zhang Er fled, saying he had old ties with the Han king and would go to Chu only because Xiang Yu had been strong enough to set him up.
translations.idiomatic: Defeated, Zhang Er fled, saying he had old ties with the Han king and would go to Chu only because Xiang Yu had been strong enough to set him up.

sentences.zh: 甘公曰：「漢王之入關，五星聚東井。

translations.literal: Gan Gong said that when the Han king entered the passes, the Five Planets gathered in Eastern Well.
translations.idiomatic: Gan Gong said that when the Han king entered the passes, the Five Planets gathered in Eastern Well.

sentences.zh: 東井者，秦分也。

translations.literal: Eastern Well belonged to Qin's celestial division.
translations.idiomatic: Eastern Well belonged to Qin's celestial division.

sentences.zh: 先至必王。

translations.literal: He who arrived first would surely become king.
translations.idiomatic: He who arrived first would surely become king.

sentences.zh: 楚雖彊，後必屬漢。」

translations.literal: So although Chu was strong, it would ultimately submit to Han.
translations.idiomatic: So although Chu was strong, it would ultimately submit to Han.

sentences.zh: 耳走漢。

translations.literal: Zhang Er therefore fled to Han.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Er therefore fled to Han.

sentences.zh: 漢亦還定三秦，方圍章邯廢丘。

translations.literal: Han had also turned back to pacify the Three Qins and was besieging Zhang Han at Feiqiu.
translations.idiomatic: Han had also turned back to pacify the Three Qins and was besieging Zhang Han at Feiqiu.

sentences.zh: 耳謁漢王，漢王厚遇之。

translations.literal: Zhang Er met the Han king, who received him generously.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Er met the Han king, who received him generously.

sentences.zh: 餘已敗耳，皆收趙地，迎趙王於代，復為趙王。

translations.literal: After defeating Zhang Er, Chen Yu recovered all Zhao territory, welcomed King Xie back from Dai, and restored him as king.
translations.idiomatic: After defeating Zhang Er, Chen Yu recovered all Zhao territory, welcomed King Xie back from Dai, and restored him as king.

sentences.zh: 趙王德餘，立以為代王。

translations.literal: King Xie, grateful to Chen Yu, made him King of Dai.
translations.idiomatic: King Xie, grateful to Chen Yu, made him King of Dai.

sentences.zh: 餘為趙王弱，國初定，留傅趙王，而使夏說以相國守代。

translations.literal: Chen Yu judged King Xie weak and the state newly settled, so he stayed as his tutor and sent Xia Yue to guard Dai as chancellor.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Yu judged King Xie weak and the state newly settled, so he stayed as his tutor and sent Xia Yue to guard Dai as chancellor.

sentences.zh: 漢二年，東擊楚，使告趙，欲與俱。

translations.literal: In Han year two, Han attacked Chu in the east and notified Zhao, seeking joint action.
translations.idiomatic: In Han year two, Han attacked Chu in the east and notified Zhao, seeking joint action.

sentences.zh: 餘曰：「漢殺張耳乃從。」

translations.literal: Chen Yu said he would comply only after Han killed Zhang Er.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Yu said he would comply only after Han killed Zhang Er.

sentences.zh: 於是漢求人類耳者，斬其頭遺餘，餘乃遣兵助漢。

translations.literal: Han then found a man who resembled Zhang Er, beheaded him, and sent the head to Chen Yu, who then dispatched troops to help Han.
translations.idiomatic: Han then found a man who resembled Zhang Er, beheaded him, and sent the head to Chen Yu, who then dispatched troops to help Han.

sentences.zh: 漢敗於彭城西，餘亦聞耳詐死，即背漢。

translations.literal: After Han's defeat west of Pengcheng, Chen Yu learned Zhang Er had only feigned death and therefore turned against Han.
translations.idiomatic: After Han's defeat west of Pengcheng, Chen Yu learned Zhang Er had only feigned death and therefore turned against Han.

sentences.zh: 漢遣耳與韓信擊破趙井陘，斬餘泜水上，追殺趙王歇襄國。

translations.literal: Han sent Zhang Er with Han Xin, who broke Zhao at Jingxing, beheaded Chen Yu at the Zhi River, and pursued and killed King Xie at Xiangguo.
translations.idiomatic: Han sent Zhang Er with Han Xin, who broke Zhao at Jingxing, beheaded Chen Yu at the Zhi River, and pursued and killed King Xie at Xiangguo.

sentences.zh: 七年，高祖從平城過趙，趙王旦暮自上食，體甚卑，有子婿禮。

translations.literal: In year seven, when Gaozu passed through Zhao from Pingcheng, the Zhao king personally served meals from morning to night with extreme humility, like a son-in-law.
translations.idiomatic: In year seven, when Gaozu passed through Zhao from Pingcheng, the Zhao king personally served meals from morning to night with extreme humility, like a son-in-law.

sentences.zh: 高祖箕踞罵詈，甚慢之。

translations.literal: Gaozu sat with legs spread, cursing and insulting him, and treated him with great contempt.
translations.idiomatic: Gaozu sat with legs spread, cursing and insulting him, and treated him with great contempt.

sentences.zh: 趙相貫高、趙午年六十餘，故耳客也，怒曰：「吾王孱王也！」

translations.literal: Zhao's chancellor Guan Gao and Zhao Wu, both over sixty and former followers of Zhang Er, grew angry and said their king was being humiliated.
translations.idiomatic: Zhao's chancellor Guan Gao and Zhao Wu, both over sixty and former followers of Zhang Er, grew angry and said their king was being humiliated.

sentences.zh: 說敖曰：「天下豪桀並起，能者先立，今王事皇帝甚恭，皇帝遇王無禮，請為王殺之。」

translations.literal: They urged Zhang Ao that in an age when heroes rose and the able took power, the emperor's disrespect deserved retaliation, and proposed killing him for the king.
translations.idiomatic: They urged Zhang Ao that in an age when heroes rose and the able took power, the emperor's disrespect deserved retaliation, and proposed killing him for the king.

sentences.zh: 敖齧其指出血，曰：「君何言之誤！

translations.literal: Zhang Ao bit his finger until it bled and said they were gravely mistaken.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Ao bit his finger until it bled and said they were gravely mistaken.

sentences.zh: 且先王亡國，賴皇帝得復國，德流子孫，秋豪皆帝力也。

translations.literal: He said his father's lost state had been restored only by the emperor, and every benefit to their descendants came from imperial power.
translations.idiomatic: He said his father's lost state had been restored only by the emperor, and every benefit to their descendants came from imperial power.

sentences.zh: 願君無復出口。」

translations.literal: He begged them never to speak of this again.
translations.idiomatic: He begged them never to speak of this again.

sentences.zh: 貫高等十餘人相謂曰：「

translations.literal: Guan Gao and more than ten others then discussed the matter among themselves.
translations.idiomatic: Guan Gao and more than ten others then discussed the matter among themselves.

sentences.zh: 吾等非也。

translations.literal: They said they, not the king, were responsible.
translations.idiomatic: They said they, not the king, were responsible.

sentences.zh: 吾王長者，不背德。

translations.literal: Their king was an honorable elder who would not betray gratitude.
translations.idiomatic: Their king was an honorable elder who would not betray gratitude.

sentences.zh: 且吾等義不辱，今帝辱我王，故欲殺之，何乃汙王為？

translations.literal: They said their own sense of honor could not bear this insult to their king, and they would not stain him by involving him.
translations.idiomatic: They said their own sense of honor could not bear this insult to their king, and they would not stain him by involving him.

sentences.zh: 事成歸王，事敗獨身坐耳。」

translations.literal: If the plan succeeded, credit would go to the king; if it failed, they alone would bear punishment.
translations.idiomatic: If the plan succeeded, credit would go to the king; if it failed, they alone would bear punishment.

sentences.zh: 八年，上從東垣過。

translations.literal: In year eight, the emperor passed through Dongyuan.
translations.idiomatic: In year eight, the emperor passed through Dongyuan.

sentences.zh: 貫高等乃壁人柏人，要之置廁。

translations.literal: Guan Gao's group secretly placed men at Bairen to strike him in a latrine.
translations.idiomatic: Guan Gao's group secretly placed men at Bairen to strike him in a latrine.

sentences.zh: 上過欲宿，心動，問曰：「縣名為何？」

translations.literal: The emperor considered lodging there, then felt uneasy and asked the county name.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor considered lodging there, then felt uneasy and asked the county name.

sentences.zh: 曰：「柏人。」

translations.literal: He was told it was Bairen.
translations.idiomatic: He was told it was Bairen.

sentences.zh: 「柏人者，迫於人！」

translations.literal: He said Bairen sounds like being forced by men.
translations.idiomatic: He said Bairen sounds like being forced by men.

sentences.zh: 不宿去。

translations.literal: So he did not stay and left.
translations.idiomatic: So he did not stay and left.

sentences.zh: 九年，貫高怨家知其謀，告之。

translations.literal: In year nine, an enemy of Guan Gao learned of the plot and reported it.
translations.idiomatic: In year nine, an enemy of Guan Gao learned of the plot and reported it.

sentences.zh: 於是上逮捕趙王諸反者。

translations.literal: The emperor then arrested Zhao king's associates implicated in rebellion.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor then arrested Zhao king's associates implicated in rebellion.

sentences.zh: 趙午等十餘人皆爭自剄，貫高獨怒罵曰：「誰令公等為之？

translations.literal: Zhao Wu and over ten others all fought to cut their own throats, but Guan Gao alone cursed them angrily.
translations.idiomatic: Zhao Wu and over ten others all fought to cut their own throats, but Guan Gao alone cursed them angrily.

sentences.zh: 今王實無謀，而并捕王；

translations.literal: He said the king had not planned any rebellion and was being arrested together with them.
translations.idiomatic: He said the king had not planned any rebellion and was being arrested together with them.

sentences.zh: 公等死，誰當白王不反者？」

translations.literal: If they all died, who would clear the king's name.
translations.idiomatic: If they all died, who would clear the king's name.

sentences.zh: 乃檻車與王詣長安。

translations.literal: He and the king were taken in caged carts to Chang'an.
translations.idiomatic: He and the king were taken in caged carts to Chang'an.

sentences.zh: 高對獄曰：「獨吾屬為之，王不知也。」

translations.literal: In interrogation, Guan Gao said only his own group had acted and the king knew nothing.
translations.idiomatic: In interrogation, Guan Gao said only his own group had acted and the king knew nothing.

sentences.zh: 吏榜笞數千，刺爇，身無完者，終不復言。

translations.literal: Officials beat him with thousands of strokes and burned him with irons; his body had no uninjured part, yet he never changed his statement.
translations.idiomatic: Officials beat him with thousands of strokes and burned him with irons; his body had no uninjured part, yet he never changed his statement.

sentences.zh: 呂后數言張王以魯元故，不宜有此。

translations.literal: Empress Lu repeatedly said Zhang Ao, because of his tie to Princess Lu Yuan, should not be treated this way.
translations.idiomatic: Empress Lu repeatedly said Zhang Ao, because of his tie to Princess Lu Yuan, should not be treated this way.

sentences.zh: 上怒曰：「使長敖據天下，豈少乃女虖！」

translations.literal: The emperor angrily replied that if Zhang Ao held the realm, it would not be only his daughter who suffered.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor angrily replied that if Zhang Ao held the realm, it would not be only his daughter who suffered.

sentences.zh: 廷尉以貫高辭聞，上曰：「壯士！

translations.literal: When the chief justice reported Guan Gao's testimony, the emperor said this was a true strong man.
translations.idiomatic: When the chief justice reported Guan Gao's testimony, the emperor said this was a true strong man.

sentences.zh: 誰知者，以私問之。」

translations.literal: He ordered that anyone who knew Guan Gao should question him privately.
translations.idiomatic: He ordered that anyone who knew Guan Gao should question him privately.

sentences.zh: 中大夫泄公曰：「臣素知之，此固趙國立名義不侵為然諾者也。」

translations.literal: Grandee Xie Gong said he knew Guan Gao well and that he was exactly the kind of Zhao man who kept his word and would not violate honor.
translations.idiomatic: Grandee Xie Gong said he knew Guan Gao well and that he was exactly the kind of Zhao man who kept his word and would not violate honor.

sentences.zh: 上使泄公持節問之箯輿前。

translations.literal: The emperor sent Xie Gong with tally-staff credentials to question Guan Gao before his prison litter.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor sent Xie Gong with tally-staff credentials to question Guan Gao before his prison litter.

sentences.zh: 卬視泄公，勞若如平生歡。

translations.literal: Guan Gao raised his head, recognized Xie Gong, and greeted him as warmly as in earlier days.
translations.idiomatic: Guan Gao raised his head, recognized Xie Gong, and greeted him as warmly as in earlier days.

sentences.zh: 與語，問張王果有謀不。

translations.literal: In conversation, Xie Gong asked whether Zhang Ao had truly plotted rebellion.
translations.idiomatic: In conversation, Xie Gong asked whether Zhang Ao had truly plotted rebellion.

sentences.zh: 高曰：「人情豈不各愛其父母妻子哉？

translations.literal: Guan Gao said everyone loves parents, wife, and children.
translations.idiomatic: Guan Gao said everyone loves parents, wife, and children.

sentences.zh: 今吾三族皆以論死，豈以王易吾親哉！

translations.literal: He said his three clans were already condemned to death, so he could never trade his own kin away for the king.
translations.idiomatic: He said his three clans were already condemned to death, so he could never trade his own kin away for the king.

sentences.zh: 顧為王實不反，獨吾等為之。」

translations.literal: In truth, the king did not rebel. Only we carried this out.
translations.idiomatic: In truth, the king did not rebel. Only we carried this out.

sentences.zh: 具道本根所以，王不知狀。

translations.literal: He laid out the full roots and circumstances in detail, showing the king had no knowledge of it.
translations.idiomatic: He laid out the full roots and circumstances in detail, showing the king had no knowledge of it.

sentences.zh: 於是泄公具以報上，上乃赦趙王。

translations.literal: Xie Gong reported all this fully to the emperor, and the emperor then pardoned the King of Zhao.
translations.idiomatic: Xie Gong reported all this fully to the emperor, and the emperor then pardoned the King of Zhao.

sentences.zh: 上賢高能自立然諾，使泄公赦之，告曰：「張王已出，上多足下，故赦足下。」

translations.literal: The emperor admired Guan Gao for holding to his pledged word. He ordered Xie Gong to release him and tell him: Zhang Wang has already been released; the emperor greatly values you, so you are pardoned.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor admired Guan Gao for holding to his pledged word. He ordered Xie Gong to release him and tell him: Zhang Wang has already been released; the emperor greatly values you, so you are pardoned.

sentences.zh: 高曰：「所以不死，白張王不反耳。

translations.literal: Guan Gao said: I stayed alive only to clear Zhang Wang of rebellion.
translations.idiomatic: Guan Gao said: I stayed alive only to clear Zhang Wang of rebellion.

sentences.zh: 今王已出，吾責塞矣。

translations.literal: Now that the king is out, my duty is fulfilled.
translations.idiomatic: Now that the king is out, my duty is fulfilled.

sentences.zh: 且人臣有篡弒之名，豈有面目復事上哉！」

translations.literal: A subject who bears the name of plotting regicide has no face left to serve the ruler again.
translations.idiomatic: A subject who bears the name of plotting regicide has no face left to serve the ruler again.

sentences.zh: 乃仰絕亢而死。

translations.literal: He then threw back his head and died by cutting his throat.
translations.idiomatic: He then threw back his head and died by cutting his throat.

sentences.zh: 敖已出，尚魯元公主如故，封為宣平侯。

translations.literal: After Zhang Ao was released, he remained married to Princess Luyuan as before and was enfeoffed as Marquis of Xuanping.
translations.idiomatic: After Zhang Ao was released, he remained married to Princess Luyuan as before and was enfeoffed as Marquis of Xuanping.

sentences.zh: 於是上賢張王諸客，皆以為諸侯相、郡守。

translations.literal: The emperor then valued the retainers of the Zhao king and appointed them as chancellors of feudal states and commandery governors.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor then valued the retainers of the Zhao king and appointed them as chancellors of feudal states and commandery governors.

sentences.zh: 語在田叔傳。

translations.literal: This account appears in the biography of Tian Shu.
translations.idiomatic: This account appears in the biography of Tian Shu.

sentences.zh: 及孝惠、高后、文、景時，張王客子孫皆為二千石。

translations.literal: By the reigns of Emperor Hui, Empress Gao, Emperor Wen, and Emperor Jing, descendants of the Zhao king's retainers were all serving at the two-thousand-shi rank.
translations.idiomatic: By the reigns of Emperor Hui, Empress Gao, Emperor Wen, and Emperor Jing, descendants of the Zhao king's retainers were all serving at the two-thousand-shi rank.

sentences.zh: 贊曰：張耳、陳餘，世所稱賢，其賓客廝役皆天下俊桀，所居國無不取卿相者。

translations.literal: Appraisal: Zhang Er and Chen Yu were praised in their age as worthy men. Even their retainers and servants were outstanding heroes of the realm, and in whatever state they lived, men from their circle rose to become chancellors and chief ministers.
translations.idiomatic: Appraisal: Zhang Er and Chen Yu were praised in their age as worthy men. Even their retainers and servants were outstanding heroes of the realm, and in whatever state they lived, men from their circle rose to become chancellors and chief ministers.

sentences.zh: 然耳、餘始居約時，相然信死，豈顧問哉！

translations.literal: Yet when Zhang Er and Chen Yu were first in hardship, they trusted each other to the death without hesitation.
translations.idiomatic: Yet when Zhang Er and Chen Yu were first in hardship, they trusted each other to the death without hesitation.

sentences.zh: 及據國爭權，卒相滅亡，何鄉者慕用之誠，後相背之盭也！

translations.literal: Once they held states and contended for power, however, they ended by destroying each other. How sincere their earlier mutual admiration was, and how perverse their later betrayal became.
translations.idiomatic: Once they held states and contended for power, however, they ended by destroying each other. How sincere their earlier mutual admiration was, and how perverse their later betrayal became.

sentences.zh: 勢利之交，古人羞之，蓋謂是矣。

translations.literal: Friendship founded on power and profit was something the ancients were ashamed of; this is exactly what they meant.
translations.idiomatic: Friendship founded on power and profit was something the ancients were ashamed of; this is exactly what they meant.
